name: Once-daily tadalafil did not improve measured endothelial dysfunction parameters in Porst 2012
statement: In PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with ED, tadalafil 5 mg once daily improved erectile and psychosocial outcomes, but the abstract reports no significant between-group differences in endothelial dysfunction parameters including biomarkers and peripheral arterial tonometric measures.
population_or_context: PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with ED, mean age 52 years.
quantitative_note: Abstract does not provide numeric endothelial estimates; it states no significant differences in endothelial dysfunction parameters were observed between groups.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports no significant differences in endothelial dysfunction parameters.

caveats: ED population rather than healthy users.
Endothelial endpoints are abstract-level and not numerically detailed.
No clinical cardiovascular outcomes were measured.
population_profile: Men with ED, mean age 52; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg for 12 weeks.
indication_context: On-label ED treatment context with exploratory endothelial measures.
endpoint_measure: Endothelial dysfunction biomarkers and peripheral arterial tonometric measures.
effect_estimate: No significant between-group endothelial dysfunction parameter differences reported in abstract.
safety_findings: Tadalafil was well tolerated; adverse events included back pain, headache, and dyspepsia.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for common daily tadalafil exposure; low for healthy cardiovascular benefit because participants had ED.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Exploratory surrogate endpoints, ED selection, and abstract-only endpoint detail.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is endpoint and population context.
